data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-887e2bd3">
    <title>housekeeping: reap orphan ddx worktrees and stale worker/execution state: part 2</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c191e91a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
DDx-managed filesystem state accumulates indefinitely. On the Fizeau project (sibling repo, /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) on 2026-05-14:
- /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ contained 9,735 inodes across ~9700+ directories. Many were .execute-bead-wt-* worktrees dating back to 2026-05-07 (a week stale). Each is a full repo checkout, multiplying repo size on disk.
- .ddx/workers/ held 35 agent-loop-<nano-id>/ directories. Most show last_activity_at older than 1 hour with no live process. They keep persisting per-attempt status.json entries.
- .ddx/executions/ held 44 attempt directories. Some have unmerged result.json state (a previous merge or worker crash left them in DU). No automatic GC.
- .ddx/attachments/ holds 355 per-bead artifact directories. They flood `git status` as untracked since nothing gitignores them.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx try` creates a worktree under `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<timestamp>-<hash>/` and only sometimes cleans up. Failure paths (worktree_lost, execution_failed, process crash) consistently leave the worktree behind. There's no startup reaper for stale worktrees.
- `ddx work` and `ddx try` write a worker status.json under .ddx/workers/agent-loop-<nano-id>/ and the writer is responsible for cleanup. Process crashes leave the directory behind; no startup reaper claims them.
- .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ is meant to be retained as evidence (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 evidence pile). But there's no retention policy: directories accumulate forever. Some land in unmerged git state after partial commits.
- .ddx/attachments/ and .ddx/run-state*.json are written by every ddx invocation, but the project's .gitignore policy for them is inconsistent (.ddx/executions is ignored; .ddx/attachments and .ddx/run-state.json appear in `git status` constantly).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a startup reaper to `ddx work` and `ddx try`: on launch, scan /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/, identify worktrees whose parent ddx-try/ddx-work process is dead (no PID match), `git worktree remove` + `rm -rf` them. Bound the scan to N=200 per launch so a cold start doesn't take forever. Configurable via DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE (default 72h).
2. Add a startup reaper for `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/`: read status.json's `pid` and `last_activity_at`; if pid is gone OR activity > 30 min stale, remove the directory.
3. Add a configurable execution-evidence retention policy:
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorkerDirs asserts that .ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/ directories whose status.json shows pid=dead or last_activity_at > 30 min stale are removed at startup.
- TestExecutionsRetention_ArchivesOldEvidence asserts that .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ entries older than DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS are moved under .ddx/executions-archive/<year>/<month>/ (or deleted if DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS=0).
- `ddx doctor` reports the count of stale worktrees, worker dirs, and execution dirs without making changes. `ddx doctor --apply` runs the reaper paths.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorkerDirs asserts that .ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/ directories whose status.json shows pid=dead or last_activity_at > 30 min stale are removed at startup.
2. TestExecutionsRetention_ArchivesOldEvidence asserts that .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ entries older than DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS are moved under .ddx/executions-archive/<year>/<month>/ (or deleted if DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS=0).
3. `ddx doctor` reports the count of stale worktrees, worker dirs, and execution dirs without making changes. `ddx doctor --apply` runs the reaper paths.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:agent, area:execution, kind:cleanup, decomposed</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-c191e91a" base-rev="905bcb63fd61e82f8b7d743cae39b91c3403a8bd" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260515T064010-6fbfbb43"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Added shared startup housekeeping in [startup_housekeeping.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-887e2bd3-20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/cli/cmd/startup_housekeeping.go:1) and wired it into `ddx work`, `ddx try`, and `ddx doctor --apply` via [execute_loop_shared.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-887e2bd3-20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:1), [try.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-887e2bd3-20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/cli/cmd/try.go:1), and [doctor.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-887e2bd3-20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/cli/cmd/doctor.go:1). It now:
- reaps stale `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*` dirs when the PID is dead or `last_activity_at` is older than 30 minutes,
- archives old `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` dirs into `.ddx/executions-archive/<year>/<month>/`,
- deletes old execution dirs instead when `DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS=0`,
- reports stale worktree/worker/execution counts in `ddx doctor` dry runs.
I also ignored `.ddx/executions-archive/` in [.gitignore](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-887e2bd3-20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/.gitignore:1).

Coverage was added in [work_startup_cleanup_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-887e2bd3-20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/cli/cmd/work_startup_cleanup_test.go:1) and [doctor_housekeeping_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-887e2bd3-20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/cli/cmd/doctor_housekeeping_test.go:1), including `TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorkerDirs`, `TestExecutionsRetention_ArchivesOldEvidence`, and doctor dry-run vs `--apply`. Verification passed with `go test ./cmd` and `lefthook run pre-commit`. Commit: `631fec75bd9fface4f62e0f5969efc46115828ea` (`fix: reap stale worker and execution startup state [ddx-887e2bd3]`).
